items.title: Disentangling frontal-striatal contributions to exploration
items.author: Matthew Ainsworth, Mark J. Buckley
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Treuting et al. present a dissociation between the way anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) and striatum use prediction errors to shape exploration in complementary ways, with ACC tracking prediction errors to reduce uncertainty regarding available choices and striatum using prediction errors to amplify value estimates.</article>

items.title: The hypothalamic “glucostat’’: CRF neurons decode energy signals
items.author: Ibukun Akinrinade, Jaideep S. Bains
items.content: <article>Food deprivation shifts the body’s metabolic priority toward glucose utilization as a primary fuel source. A study by Kim et al. published in this issue of Neuron identifies a distinct population of neurons in the brain as essential for detecting this metabolic state and orchestrating adaptive responses.</article>

items.title: Epigenetic signature of depression susceptibility in cortical astrocytes
items.author: Yihui Cui
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Fulton et al. discover ZBTB7A, a chromatin regulator in orbitofrontal astrocytes, drives neuronal hyperactivity to promote depression susceptibility.</article>

items.title: Immunoregulator multitasking in neurons is not just a WHIM
items.author: Philip M. Murphy
items.content: <article>Distinguishing direct from indirect mechanisms is often difficult in multisystem genetic conditions. In this issue of Neuron, Demenego et al. report that cerebellar malformation and dysfunction in WHIM syndrome immunodeficiency result from direct effects of hyperfunctional CXCR4 in neurons.</article>
